info.title: ysc
info.description:
Yarn Spinner is a tool for creating interactive dialogue for games. 
      
Write your conversations in Yarn, a simple programming language that's designed to be easy for writers to learn, while also powerful enough to handle whatever you need.

This package provides the command-line tool 'ysc', which can compile and work with Yarn Spinner scripts.

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: Command.Parse, OK: Command.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: ParseResult.Invoke, OK: ParseErrorAction.Invoke, OK: HelpAction.Invoke, OK: ParseResult.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: Compiles, runs and analyses Yarn code.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

options.name: --output-directory
options.description: Output directory (default: current directory) [default: <path>]

options.name: --output-name
options.description: Output name to use for the files (default: Output) [default: Output]

options.name: --output-string-table-name
options.description: Output string table filename (default: {name}-Lines.csv

options.name: --output-metadata-table-name
options.description: Output metadata table filename (default: {name}-Metadata.csv

options.name: --stdout
options.description: Output machine-readable compilation result to stdout instead of to files [default: False]

arguments.name: inputs
arguments.description: The .yarnproject file to compile, or a collection of .yarn files to compile.

commands.name: list-sources
commands.description: Lists Yarn sources for a Yarn project.

arguments.name: yarnproject
arguments.description: The .yarnproject file to list .yarn sources for.

commands.name: run
commands.description: Runs Yarn scripts in an interactive manner

options.name: --start-node
options.description: Name of the node to start running [default: Start]

options.name: --auto-advance
options.description: Auto-advance regular dialogue lines [default: False]

options.name: --allow-preview-features
options.description: Allow using in-development compiler features. [default: False]

arguments.name: inputs
arguments.description: The .yarnproject file to run, or a collection of .yarn files to run. One of the specified files must contain the start node.

commands.name: print-tree
commands.description: Parses a Yarn script and produces a human-readable syntax tree.

options.name: --output-directory
options.description: Output directory (default: current directory) [default: <path>]

options.name: --json
options.description: Output as JSON (default: false) [default: False]

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: The .yarnproject file indicating files to generate a parse tree from, or a collection of .yarn files.

commands.name: print-tokens
commands.description: Parses a Yarn script and produces list of parsed tokens.

options.name: --output-directory
options.description: Output directory (default: current directory) [default: <path>]

options.name: --json
options.description: Output as JSON (default: false) [default: False]

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: The .yarnproject file to generate a token list from, or a collection of .yarn files.

commands.name: tag
commands.description: Tags a Yarn script with localisation line IDs

options.name: --output-directory
options.description: Output directory to write the newly tagged files (default: override the input files)

arguments.name: inputs
arguments.description: The .yarnproject file indicating files to add tags to, or a collection of .yarn files

commands.name: extract
commands.description: Extracts strings from the provided files

options.name: --format
options.description: The export file format for the extracted strings, defaults to csv [default: csv]

options.name: --columns
options.description: The desired columns in the exported table of strings. [default: System.String[]]

options.name: --default-name
options.description: The default character name to use. Defaults to none.

options.name: --output
options.description: File location for saving the extracted strings. Defaults to a file named lines in the current directory

arguments.name: inputs
arguments.description: The .yarnproject file indicating Yarn files to extract strings from, or a collection of .yarn files.

commands.name: graph
commands.description: Exports a graph view of the dialogue

options.name: --output
options.description: File location for saving the graph. Defaults to a file named dialogue in the current directory

options.name: --format
options.description: The graph format, defaults to dot [default: dot]

options.name: --clustering
options.description: Generate a graph with clustering subgraphs (default: false) [default: False]

arguments.name: inputs
arguments.description: The .yarnproject file indicating Yarn files to export a graph of, or a collection of .yarn files.

commands.name: browse-binary
commands.description: Browses some of the common data inside of a compiled yarn program

arguments.name: compiledInput
arguments.description: The .yarnc file you want to browse

commands.name: create-proj
commands.description: Creates a yarn project file with default values

options.name: --unity-exclusion
options.description: Set the excludeFiles value to ignore folders with trailing ~ (default: false) [default: False]

commands.name: dump-code
commands.description: Compiles the specified input, and prints the disassembled code.

options.name: --allow-preview-features
options.description: Allow using in-development compiler features. [default: False]

arguments.name: inputs
arguments.description: The .yarnproject file to compile, or a collection of .yarn files to compile.

commands.name: generate-definitions
commands.description: Generates YSLS files for your project.

options.name: --output-directory
options.description: Output directory (default: current directory) [default: <path>]

options.name: --project-type
options.description: The engine this project uses. Used to determine the engine specific variations of the generated YSLS file. [default: Unity]

arguments.name: inputDirectory
arguments.description: The directory of the project containing your implementation files
